name: Serralada Litoral Park Loop from Montcada
name_original: Ruta circular en Serralada Marina desde Montcada
description: This difficult 28.8-mile mountain biking route in Serralada Marina offers panoramic views of Barcelona and technical terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Montcada I Reixac offers diverse terrain at the confluence of the Ripoll and Besòs rivers and at the foot of the Collserola Natural Park. This region provides a mix of riverine landscapes and mountainous trails. Riders can explore extensive networks within Collserola Natural Park, featuring varied elevation and natural features. The area includes both paved and unpaved segments, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails in Montcada I Reixac The most popular mountain bike trail is Serralada Litoral Park Loop from Montcada, a 28.8 miles (46.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through the Serralada Litoral Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sant Pere de Reixac Loop, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This route explores the area around the historic Sant Pere de Reixac church. Local mountain bikers also love the Can Magí Spring – View of La Mola loop from Montcada i Reixac - Manresa, a 40.2 miles (64.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Montcada I Reixac is defined by the trails of Collserola Natural Park, the riverside paths along the Ripoll and Besòs rivers, and diverse terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy river routes to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Montcada I Reixac are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Montcada I Reixac's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Montcada i Reixac?
A: Montcada i Reixac offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from riverine paths along the Ripoll and Besòs rivers to more challenging mountainous terrain within the Collserola Natural Park. You'll find a mix of paved and unpaved segments, catering to various skill levels. The region is known for its extensive trail networks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Montcada i Reixac?
A: Yes, there are options for beginners and families. The riverside paths along the Ripoll and Besòs rivers offer easier, more relaxed rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39554071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Zam Park loop from Montcada-Ripollet&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route with minimal elevation gain, suitable for those looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What are the more challenging mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes, particularly within the Collserola Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958697056/serralada-litoral-park-loop-from-montcada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serralada Litoral Park Loop from Montcada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 46.3 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Magí Spring – View of La Mola loop from Montcada i Reixac - Manresa&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 64 km with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for mountain biking in Collserola Natural Park?
A: Collserola Natural Park has specific regulations to protect its environment. While general access for cycling is permitted on designated trails, it's always advisable to check the official Collserola Natural Park website for the latest information on specific rules, restricted areas, or any permit requirements for certain activities or events. Respecting signage and trail etiquette is crucial.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. Within Collserola Natural Park, you can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5109965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de Valldaura Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939850476/sant-pere-de-reixac-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Reixac Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the area around the historic Sant Pere de Reixac church, blending natural beauty with heritage. The Turó de Montcada also provides a natural viewpoint overlooking Barcelona and the Besòs delta.

Q: How accessible are the mountain bike trails by public transport?
A: Montcada i Reixac is well-integrated into the Barcelona metropolitan area, making it relatively accessible by public transport. Many trailheads are within reach of local train stations or bus stops, especially those closer to the town center or along the river paths. This allows for convenient access for riders without private vehicles.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Montcada i Reixac are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958697056/serralada-litoral-park-loop-from-montcada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serralada Litoral Park Loop from Montcada&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939850476/sant-pere-de-reixac-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Reixac Loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploring the area.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Montcada i Reixac?
A: The mountain biking experience in Montcada i Reixac is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of riverine landscapes with mountainous trails, and the extensive network of paths within Collserola Natural Park. The accessibility from Barcelona is also a frequently appreciated aspect.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural parks like Collserola, but they must be kept on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other visitors. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Specific rules might apply to certain protected zones, so checking local park guidelines is recommended.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails?
A: Montcada i Reixac, being a populated area, offers various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel before or after your ride. Many establishments are located in the town center or along main roads, often accessible from the riverside paths. For trails deeper into Collserola, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Montcada i Reixac?
A: The region generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are often considered ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for riding. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon rides are preferable. Winters are generally mild, making year-round riding possible, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Montcada i Reixac?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails documented in Montcada i Reixac on komoot, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulties. This includes 3 easy, 7 moderate, and 7 difficult routes, ensuring options for every skill level.
tips: Bridge for the exclusive use of pedestrians or bicycles, ideal for crossing the Besós River from the municipality of La Llagosta to the Parc de la Serralada de Marina or vice versa, for walking or doing any sporting activity.

Quite a considerable drop due to the crossing of the Passeig Fluvial Riu Besòs with a huge ditch or channel about 7 meters wide by about 2 meters deep.

   It can be easily crossed by bicycle, but it is necessary to slow down a bit because both the entrance and the exit of this channel are covered with stones.

The Font d'en Mamet de Vilassar de Dalt is located in the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, which is located in the Molí d'en Cuquet stream, a few meters above the entrance to Can Mamet, at the foot of the track forest that leads from Can Maimó to Creu d'en Boquet.

    It is a very rustic fountain: only the spout embedded in the rock, some wooden railings and four steps dug into the ground to go down to the natural pond where the water is collected. Due to the fact of being in the bed of the torrent d'en Cuquet (which maintains a good level of humidity and more or less flow throughout the year) the source flows almost always. Unfortunately, the water is not drinkable. Recently it has been fixed: they have cleared it, repaired the steps that go down and replaced the spout. The water flows through a tube embedded in the stones of the bank, a foot high, and falls to the ground forming a small pool that drains directly into the stream. To the right of the fountain, at right angles to it, there is a terrace attached to the margin. Above the fountain we find a sign with the name of the fountain and a series of warnings.

La Creu de Can Boquet is a 378-meter mountain pass that connects the hills of Roure and Pedrells in the municipality of Vilassar de Dalt, in the Maresme region.

It marks a crossroads where, in addition to the Pi de la Creu de Can Boquet, there is an information point of the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. The Roca d'en Toni dolmen is nearby.

This space for rest and privileged viewpoint at the foot of Sant Mateu, is enabled with benches and parking support for bikes, and car parking. There is a panoramic sign that locates, with the corresponding place names, the hills and peaks that surround this point of view, both in the Maresme and Barcelonès, and in the Vallès Oriental and Occidental. From this point you can see the mountains of Montjuïc and Tibidabo, La Conreria, the Teià valley and the hills on the Vallesana slope, as well as being able to contemplate Montserrat, La Mola and Sant Llorenç del Munt.
   The viewpoint was inaugurated in 2004.
Spectacular views of a large part of the Maresme and Barcelonés coastal regions.

The Pere Tarrés Foundation founded in 1985 by the Archdiocese of Barcelona is a non-profit organization of social and educational action, dedicated to the field of education in children's and youth free time and volunteering; These areas of action are based on the promotion of education in free time, culture, associative life, socio-cultural animation and social education. Currently its action, in addition to nurseries, has been extended to training and research.

Emblematic crossroads and meeting point for cyclists and hikers, where the main track that goes up from the Mirador de la Vallençana towards the Turó La Coscollada converge, with the trail that runs parallel to the quarry from the Turó de Les Maleses and with the path that goes down to the Font de l'Amigó.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Ruta circular en Serralada Marina desde Montcada is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride. It requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to significant climbs, technical descents, and sections with loose, slippery stone. Some parts may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain, including wide tracks ('pista ancha'), challenging climbs ('cuestas importantes'), and technical singletrack sections known as 'trialeras'. You'll encounter areas with 'mucha piedra suelta y resbaladizo' (lots of loose and slippery stone), making some descents quite technical. There are also fast, pedalable sections of track.

faqs.question: What notable sights or viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: This route provides impressive views of Barcelona and nearly 360-degree panoramic vistas that include Tibidabo, Montserrat, Montjuïc, and the sea. Key highlights include the Summit of El Telégrafo, Cornice Viewpoint, and View of Barcelona and the Coast. You'll also pass natural and historical points like Font d'en Mamet and Fundación Pere Tarrés.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Montcada i Reixac. You can typically find parking in or around the town center, which provides convenient access to the starting point of this circular route. Specific parking areas may vary, so checking local maps for public parking lots is recommended.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Yes, Montcada i Reixac is well-connected by public transport. You can reach the town by train (Rodalies de Catalunya) or bus, making it accessible without a car. From the station or bus stop, the starting point of the route is usually within cycling distance.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta circular en Serralada Marina desde Montcada dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Parc de la Serralada Litoral, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and other trail users. This particular route is long and challenging, so consider your dog's fitness and ability to handle the distance and technical terrain before bringing them along.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making the challenging climbs even more demanding. Winter can bring cooler temperatures and potentially slippery conditions on technical sections, especially after rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride in Parc de la Serralada Litoral?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails within Parc de la Serralada Litoral. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of other users and the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official mountain bike trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Notable sections include parts of the GR 92 BTT, La Jonquera - Sant Adrià del Besós, Montgat-Tordera, and Catalunya E17, among others. This offers opportunities to extend or modify your ride if you're familiar with the area.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route for refreshments?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Montcada i Reixac, you'll find various cafes, bars, and restaurants in the town where you can refuel before or after your ride. Along the trail itself, options are limited, so it's best to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes/patch kit), a first-aid kit, and appropriate mountain biking gear. A helmet is essential, and protective pads are highly recommended for the technical descents. A fully charged phone and a GPS device with the route loaded are also crucial.

waypoints.name: View of Barcelona and the Coast
